title: Design Review
use_when: Use Design Review when a product flow, mockup, implemented UI, design handoff, or experience change needs a gate decision on usability, hierarchy, states, accessibility, responsiveness, and design-system fit.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Design Review for market thesis, requirements scope, backend architecture, QA execution, release mechanics, documentation, DX, or stakeholder messaging unless the core risk is product experience quality.
output_artifact: Design review with experience readout, blocking issues, fixes, decision, and handoff
framework_fit: Design Review fits the product-experience gate before build, after implementation, or before release when visual hierarchy, interaction quality, accessibility, responsive behavior, and handoff clarity can make or break the product outcome.
failure_modes: Reviews only surface aesthetics while missing interaction states, accessibility, responsive behavior, edge cases, and downstream handoff requirements.
Approves design work without concrete acceptance checks that engineering and QA can verify in the implemented product.
Reopens product strategy or architecture questions instead of escalating them to thesis, product, or engineering gates when design evidence exposes them.

examples.prompt: Use $design-review to evaluate this implemented UI for hierarchy, states, accessibility, responsive behavior, and handoff blockers before release.
examples.expected_artifact: Design review with experience readout, blocking issues, fixes, decision, and handoff
